behavior_summary: Defines the behaviour for the NPC Bentic (librarian) covering: what happens when the player 'looks' at the NPC, uses/interacts with the NPC, when the NPC is scheduled to act, how it reacts to actors entering/leaving its immediate trigger region (fast-area), how it responds to theft by the avatar, and how it reacts when attacked or targeted by spells.

BENTIC::look (main) presents a short identification string to the player. If the NPC is dead it emits a dead-librarian line; otherwise it composes a short identification from global flags (benticName, benticJob) to emit either 'Bentic the Librarian', 'Bentic', 'librarian', or 'man'.

BENTIC::use manages a conversational menu: greeting variants depend on benticMet and haveAvaName, it clears mentionedPagan at start, asks dialog choices via Item::ask, records whether the player gave their name, sets benticMet/benticName/haveAvaName appropriately, and conditionally offers a book or a story (benticGivenBook, benticStoryTold affect branches).
BENTIC::schedule periodically queries FREE::28F9 to compute schedule parameters; if alive it spawns METHOD::133F to perform scheduled movement/action.

BENTIC::enterFastArea/leaveFastArea implement proximity-trigger behavior: enterFastArea performs housekeeping (FREE::2A56) then spawns a reaction routine (U8P_BENTIC::3A25) when alive; leaveFastArea runs housekeeping and, if alive, teleports the NPC to a nearby random location using urandom + Npc::teleport.

BENTIC::AvatarStoleSomething detects when the avatar steals certain item types (via Item::getType), resets stored references (resetRef), barks a plea to return the item, and spawns the enterFastArea handler so the NPC can react/move toward the actor.

BENTIC::cast handles being targeted/attacked: it checks for an attacker target, uses randomness (urandom) to decide whether to bark for help and/or spawn a guard-alert (CALGUARD::0080); when no direct target it spawns FREE::1C19 as a reaction helper.
notable_control_flow: Npc::isDead short-circuits multiple handlers: U8P_BENTIC::look(), schedule(), enterFastArea() and others avoid normal behavior when the NPC is dead (dead-specific dialog or immediate returns).

Dialog branching in BENTIC::use is driven by global flags (benticMet, benticName, haveAvaName, benticGivenBook, benticStoryTold, mentionedPagan) and by the player choice string returned from Item::ask.

Asynchronous/spawned behavior is used for movement and reactions: schedule() spawns METHOD::133F; enterFastArea() spawns U8P_BENTIC::3A25; AvatarStoleSomething spawns a facing/animation helper (METHOD::15E7) and enterFastArea; cast() may spawn CALGUARD::0080 or FREE::1C19.
Randomness (urandom) is used both for teleport offset computation in leaveFastArea and for probabilistic decisions in cast() about calling for help or alerting guards.
Item::ask returns a string result which is strcmp'd against known options to choose conversation branches; responses update global state flags to affect future interactions.
open_questions: Exact semantics and bit meanings returned by FREE::28F9 (calendar/availability bits) — the schedule() logic uses them to compute parameters but the concrete mapping of bits to times/actions is not present.

Precise identity and content of the options presented in Item::ask (strings) and the full set of dialog lines; we have inferred branch labels but not literal dialog beyond 'Bentic the Librarian' and similar identifiers.

Which concrete item types AvatarStoleSomething tests for (Item::getType compares to constants) — the types are referenced but the constants' human-readable names are not included in the canonical extract.
Whether and under what exact conditions benticGivenBook is set when a book is given — observed reads and likely writes exist but the exact assignment location/mechanism is inferred.
The exact behavior and parameters of spawned METHOD::133F and U8P_BENTIC::3A25 (movement vs animation vs state change) beyond the inferred roles.

main_function.name: U8P_BENTIC::look
main_function.purpose: When a player looks at the NPC, produce an appropriate short identification line. If the NPC is dead emit a dead-specific line; otherwise build and emit an identification that may include the NPC's personal name and/or job based on global flags.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — triggered when the Avatar (player) looks at this NPC

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::look
function_map.role: Handle 'U8P_BENTIC::look' (examine) action: present short identification or dead line to the viewer; consult benticName and benticJob to choose phrasing.

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic scheduler: read calendar/availability via FREE::28F9, assemble parameters and spawn METHOD::133F to perform scheduled activity (movement/animation).

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area 'enter' trigger: housekeeping via FREE::2A56, then spawn a reaction routine (U8P_BENTIC::3A25) if NPC is alive so it can respond/move.

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area 'leave' trigger: always call FREE::2A56; if NPC alive compute random offset (urandom) and teleport NPC nearby (Npc::teleport).

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::AvatarStoleSomething
function_map.role: React to avatar theft: check stolen item's type (Item::getType), reset stored references (resetRef), bark a plea to return the item, spawn follow-up reaction (enterFastArea) and short animation (METHOD::15E7).

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::cast
function_map.role: Reaction to spells/attacks: check for attacker/target, use urandom for probabilistic decisions to bark for help or 'Help! Sorcerer!', and possibly spawn a guard-alert (CALGUARD::0080) or a reaction helper (FREE::1C19).

function_map.name: U8P_BENTIC::use
function_map.role: Primary player interaction menu: manage greetings, ask questions (Item::ask), record player name and 'met' state, offer topics (name/reading/job/story) and conditionally offer/take actions around books and stories.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn a reaction/movement routine when an actor enters the NPC's fast-area trigger (NPC reacts/approaches).
After reacting to theft (barking, resetting refs), trigger the enterFastArea logic so NPC will follow or respond; used to sequence theft reaction into movement/behavior.

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the NPC is dead to choose dead-specific U8P_BENTIC::look text or normal identification.
Emit the short identification string to the viewing player/process.
Read calendar/availability or scheduling bits used to construct parameters for scheduled actions.
Spawn the scheduled action (movement/animation/state change) with parameters assembled from FREE::28F9 results.
Housekeeping or state check when an actor enters the fast-area before spawning reactions.
Housekeeping/state helper invoked on leaving the fast-area.
Generate random word(s) to compute position offsets for teleportation.
Teleport the NPC to a computed nearby coordinate when the fast-area is left.
Determine stolen item's type for conditional handling (which items trigger the theft reaction).
Clear stored ownership or map references affected by the theft.
Bark a plea to the avatar to return the stolen item.
Get NPC facing to spawn a short facing/animation helper for theft reaction.
Perform a brief facing/animation action based on NPC direction after a theft.
Check whether the NPC has an attacker/target to tailor cast/attack responses.
Make probabilistic decisions about shouting for help or spawning guards.
Alert guards or trigger guard-related behavior when the NPC calls for help.
Spawn a helper reaction when cast() is invoked without a direct attacker/target (timed/auxiliary reaction).
Present dialog choices to the player and receive a string result which drives branching in the interaction menu.
Retrieve the player's/Avatar's name for personalized responses when the name is known or just provided.

reads.name: benticName
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether the NPC includes its personal name in U8P_BENTIC::look/use responses and which identification string to present.

reads.name: benticJob
reads.likely_effect: Controls whether the NPC includes its job title ('librarian') in U8P_BENTIC::look/use responses.

reads.name: benticMet
reads.likely_effect: Indicates whether the NPC has previously met the player; affects greeting branches in use().

reads.name: mentionedPagan
reads.likely_effect: Used in dialog branching inside use(); cleared at the start of use() to influence which conversation options are shown.

reads.name: haveAvaName
reads.likely_effect: Indicates whether the player's name is known; read to determine if dialog should be personalized.

reads.name: benticStoryTold
reads.likely_effect: Prevents repeating a particular story by determining whether the story option should be offered in use().

reads.name: benticGivenBook
reads.likely_effect: Indicates whether the NPC has already given its book to the player; used to suppress or alter book-offer dialog.

writes.name: mentionedPagan
writes.likely_effect: Set to 0 at the start of use() to clear any prior mention state; affects subsequent dialog options in the current interaction.

writes.name: benticMet
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the player provides their name or completes certain greeting branches to record that the NPC has met the player.

writes.name: benticName
writes.likely_effect: Set in some use() branches to remember that the NPC's name token should be used in future U8P_BENTIC::look/use outputs.

writes.name: haveAvaName
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the player tells their name so future dialog can be personalized.

writes.name: benticGivenBook
writes.likely_effect: May be set when the NPC gives its book to the player, preventing repeated offers; exact write location is inferred.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Defines the behaviour for the NPC Bentic (librarian) covering: what happens when the player 'looks' at the NPC, uses/interacts with the NPC, when the NPC is scheduled to act, how it reacts to actors entering/leaving its immediate trigger region (fast-area), how it responds to theft by the avatar, and how it reacts when attacked or targeted by spells.
